title: Phacelia patuliflora
taxonomy: Phacelia patuliflora is classified within the plant family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. This family comprises about 100 genera and over 2,000 species of flowering plants. Boraginaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Phacelia patuliflora belonging to the Heliotideae subfamily. Within the genus Phacelia, P. patuliflora is a member of the Phacelia group, which is native to the Americas. The classification of P. patuliflora reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
uses: Phacelia patuliflora, also known as wild fennel or creeping phacelia, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is rich in antioxidants and has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and stems are used to make teas and infusions that can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Additionally, Phacelia patuliflora has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

properties: Phacelia patuliflora, also known as Lacy Phacelia or Wild Heliotrope, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including its antifungal and antibacterial activities. intro: Phacelia patuliflora, commonly known as blue dwarf phacelia or creeping phacelia, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Phacelia patuliflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Phacelia patuliflora is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory problems.
